title: Definitions in the Code to be understood subject to exceptions
content: Throughout this Code every definition of an offence, every penal provision and every illustration of every such definition or penal provision, shall be understood subject to the exceptions contained in the chapter entitled 'General Exceptions,' though those exceptions are not repeated in such definition, penal provision or illustration.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A child under seven years of age commits an act that would otherwise be an offence.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: Even though the definition of the offence does not explicitly state it, the act is not an offence because it is understood subject to the General Exception for children under seven.

title: Sense of expression once explained
content: Every expression which is explained in any part of this Code is used in every part of this Code in conformity with the explanation.

title: Gender
content: The pronoun 'he' and its derivatives are used of any person, whether male or female.

title: Number
content: Unless the contrary appears from the context, words importing the singular number include the plural number, and words importing the plural number include the singular number.

title: Man, Woman
content: The word 'man' denotes a male human being of any age; the word 'woman' denotes a female human being of any age.

title: Person
content: The word 'person' includes any Company or Association or body of persons, whether incorporated or not.

title: Public
content: The word 'public' includes any class of the public or any community.

title: Definition of Queen
content: [Repealed by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981 (27 of 1981)].
tap_to_reveal_footnote: Section 13 was repealed as it became redundant following the establishment of the Republic.

title: Servant of the State
content: The words 'servant of the State' denote any officer or servant continued, appointed or employed in Pakistan by or under the authority of the Government.

title: Definition of British India
content: [Repealed by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981 (27 of 1981)].

title: Definition of Government of India
content: [Repealed by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981 (27 of 1981)].

title: Government
content: The word 'Government' denotes the Federal Government or the Provincial Government.

title: India
content: [Omitted by the Federal Laws (Revision and Declaration) Ordinance, 1981 (27 of 1981)].

title: Judge
content: The word 'Judge' denotes not only every person who is officially designated as a Judge, but also every person who is empowered by law to give, in any legal proceeding, civil or criminal, a definitive judgment.

title: Court of Justice
content: The words 'Court of Justice' denote a Judge who is empowered by law to act judicially alone.

title: Public servant
content: The words 'public servant' denote a person falling under any of the descriptions following: Commissioned Officers, Judges, Court Officers, Jurors, Arbitrators, Officers of confinement, Officers of Government duty-bound to prevent offences, Officers of Government duty-bound to manage property.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A Municipal Commissioner performing official duties.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: Qualifies as a public servant under the Seventh description, as it is their duty to protect public health, safety, or convenience.

title: Moveable property
content: Corporeal property of every description, except land and things attached to the earth.

title: Wrongful gain / Wrongful loss
content: 'Wrongful gain' is gain by unlawful means of property to which the person gaining is not legally entitled. 'Wrongful loss' is loss by unlawful means of property to which the person losing it is legally entitled.

title: Dishonestly
content: Intention of causing wrongful gain to one person or wrongful loss to another person.

title: Fraudulently
content: Intent to defraud but not otherwise.

title: Reason to believe
content: Sufficient cause to believe that thing but not otherwise.

title: Property in possession of wife, clerk or servant
content: Possession by wife, clerk or servant on account of a person is possession of that person.

title: Counterfeit
content: Causes one thing to resemble another, intending by that resemblance to practice deception.

title: Document
content: Any matter expressed or described upon any substance by means of letters, figures, or marks.

title: Valuable security
content: A document whereby any legal right is created, extended, transferred, restricted, extinguished or released.

title: A will
content: Any testamentary document.

title: Words referring to acts include illegal omissions
Acts done by several persons in furtherance of common intention
When such an act is criminal by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention
Effect caused partly by act and partly by omission
Co-operation by doing one of several acts constituting an offence
Persons concerned in criminal act may be guilty of different offences
Good faith
content: Nothing is said to be done or believed in 'good faith' which is done or believed without due care and attention.
